hook.text: Google stores billions of lines of code in one repository. One commit can touch frontend, backend, and infra.

buildup.text: In a monorepo, running the full pipeline on every commit wastes time. Most changes only affect one service.

discovery.text: Affected-file detection solves this. The pipeline checks which files changed and only builds those services.

twist.text: Dependency graphs add intelligence. If a shared library changes, rebuild every service that imports it.

climax.text: Nx, Turborepo, and Bazel are monorepo build tools. They cache, parallelize, and only rebuild what's needed.

punchline.text: Monorepo pipelines build smart: only what changed, plus dependents.

quiz.question: How do monorepo pipelines avoid rebuilding everything on each commit?
options: By detecting which files changed and building only affected services
By splitting the repo into separate repositories
By running builds only once a day
